issues.description: The pause-and-try pair moved earlier (now 12/13, right after the product rule in 11) — good. But my prior review asked for a SECOND pause after the quotient rule on something like d/dx[(x^2+1)/x], because quotient rule is the most error-prone rule in the lesson and now has zero active-recall checkpoint. Right now a student practices product rule (the easier of the two) and never tests themselves on the harder one before the recap.
issues.fix: Add a quick pause-and-try after 15_compare on a small quotient like d/dx[(x^2+1)/x] or d/dx[sin x / x^2]. Doesn't need to be a full 2-slide pause/reveal — even a 'try it, then I'll show the answer' single slide is enough. Won't damage the lesson if left as-is, but the asymmetry between which rule gets practiced and which gets skipped is the kind of thing a sharp student notices.

issues.description: Still two separate outro slides at the end. With 18 sections in 10 minutes and the pause moved earlier, the back-end is less crowded than before, so this is much less of a problem — but ending on a path/CTA slide rather than the recap still means the last beat the student hears is administrative (readings, dashboard, advisor check-in) rather than mathematical. Minor pacing nit; not a misconception risk.
issues.fix: Optional: merge into a single closing slide with the recap content as the headline and the path as a footer strip. Pass-level if left alone.

overall_comment: The critical issue is resolved. The 08_diff_implies_cont slide now covers all four failure modes — corner (|x|), cusp (x^(2/3)), vertical tangent (x^(1/3)), and discontinuity — exactly the FUN 2.4 spread the AP exam tests. The dy/dx framing in 06 now plants the chain-rule bridge instead of contradicting it, 07 explicitly flags symmetric difference quotient as an approximation (not the definition), the quotient-rule mnemonic in 14 now matches the formula's left-to-right order, the trig minus-sign pattern in 16 is tightened to the three specific co-functions, the pause-and-try moved to immediately after the product rule, and ln x got its domain footnote in 10. Two minor smoothing items remain — no pause to practice quotient rule, and the recap-then-path outro still ends on a CTA — but neither plants a misconception. Ship it.
